unassigned: View reservations without a room assignment.

operation: How do I generate business report data?
orders: How do I handle in-house reservations?
roomStatus: How do I configure room and room-type status?

orders: Open reservations and continue arrivals, departures, and pending work.
channels: Review connections, mappings, and channel operations.
statistics: Review business performance, reports, and the data center.
reviews: Handle guest registrations and review tasks.
messages: Review guest conversations and unread messages.
systemNotifications: Review system, housekeeping, and task notifications.
orderNotifications: Handle reservation alerts and unread notifications.
wallet: Review balance, transactions, withdrawals, and verification.
profile: Update your name, avatar, gender, and password.
settings: Manage property, account, and business configuration.
customize: Choose which shortcuts appear on Home.

addAccount.title: Airbnb Direct Connection
addAccount.descriptionSuffix: for details. Note: only the owner account can authorize.
addAccount.paragraph1: Airbnb direct connection means THE HOST HUB establishes a system connection with Airbnb after you authorize the owner account. You can manage listings, rates, availability, and orders through the PMS.
addAccount.paragraph2: Before enabling Airbnb direct connection, read and accept the user notice, especially liability limits, dispute resolution, and applicable-law terms.
addAccount.feature1: 1.1 After connection, Airbnb rates, inventory, room status, and orders can be managed through THE HOST HUB PMS.
addAccount.feature2: 1.2 One THE HOST HUB account can connect multiple Airbnb accounts. Make sure you are signed out of Airbnb before authorization to avoid linking the wrong account.
addAccount.feature3: 1.3 Some listing types can map to an entire room type with multiple inventory units; other listing types can map only to one room.

addHotel.title: Connect {name}
addHotel.headerDescription: {name} direct connection requires channel account, hotel profile, and authorization requirements to be confirmed first. Continuing means you understand the onboarding notes.
addHotel.registrationNotice: Note: if you do not have a {name} merchant account, complete registration and hotel profile setup in {name} first.
addHotel.serviceDescription: {name} direct connection establishes a system connection between THE HOST HUB and {name} after channel approval and account authorization, used to sync hotels, rooms, rates, inventory, and orders.
addHotel.enableNotice: Before enabling {name} direct connection, confirm that you have permission to manage the channel account and hotel resources.
addHotel.functionScope: After real authorization and profile matching, the system can sync hotels, room types, rate plans, inventory, rates, and orders according to channel support.
addHotel.preparation: Complete hotel creation, profile review, and required room/rate setup in {name}, and make sure this account has authorization or management permissions.
addHotel.nextStep: After clicking continue, this page will close and guide you to the real authorization or operations onboarding flow. It will not fake authorization success or write simulated hotels.

bookingSettings.title: Booking Settings
bookingSettings.advanceBookingDesc: How many hours in advance do guests need to book?
bookingSettings.approvalDesc: Bookings not completed within your invitation window will become booking requests.
bookingSettings.preparationDesc: Maximum gap between booking date and check-in date.
bookingSettings.bookingWindowDesc: Maximum gap between booking date and check-in date.

connectionHistory.title: Connection History
connectionHistory.tip: Use the standalone channel-simulator to verify simulated connection flows.

connectNotice.title: {name} Direct Connection Notice
connectNotice.description: After authorizing your {name} account, you can start direct connection. Continuing means you have read and agreed to the notice.
connectNotice.paragraph1: {name} direct connection establishes a system connection between THE HOST HUB and {name}, helping reduce operations work and improve efficiency.
connectNotice.paragraph2: Please read this notice carefully before enabling the service. If you do not accept the terms, stop using the service.
connectNotice.feature1: 1.1 After connection, channel rates, room status, and channel orders can be managed through THE HOST HUB PMS.
connectNotice.feature2: 1.2 One THE HOST HUB account can connect multiple {name} accounts. Make sure the correct channel account is authorized.
connectNotice.feature3: 1.3 Mapping rules depend on the listing type and the channel account permissions.
connectNotice.changeText: The platform may update this notice from time to time. Continued use means you accept the changes.
connectNotice.disclaimerText: You understand that network services may be affected by force majeure, system instability, network failures, maintenance, or other uncontrollable factors.
connectNotice.lawText: Disputes related to this notice should be resolved through friendly negotiation first, then through the competent court if negotiation fails.

messages.disconnectHotelConfirm: Disconnect hotel "{name}"? Orders and room status for this hotel will no longer sync after disconnection.
messages.airbnbAuthNotice: The production channel management page has not configured Airbnb OAuth authorization yet. It will not open placeholder authorization URLs, fake authorization success, or write simulated accounts. Use the standalone channel-simulator for full-flow verification.
messages.channelAuthNotice: The production channel management page will not run simulated authorization or write simulated hotels. Use the standalone channel-simulator for full-flow verification.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to THE HOST HUB. These terms are entered into by the platform operator and you, including hotels, homestays, apartments, and their authorized users, and have legal effect.

sections.title: 1. Scope
paragraphs: These terms apply when you access, register for, sign in to, and use store management, room status management, order processing, channel management, account permission management, notifications, and related services.

sections.title: 2. Account Registration and Use
paragraphs: 1. You must provide true, accurate, and complete registration information and keep it updated.
2. You must properly keep your account, password, verification code, and login credentials, and are responsible for all actions under your account.
3. Without our written permission, you may not rent, lend, transfer, or otherwise illegally obtain or use another person's account.

sections.title: 3. Services and Use Rules
paragraphs: 1. During the valid service period, you may use enabled modules actually provided by the system.
2. You must comply with applicable laws and may not use the system for illegal activities, including publishing illegal information, maliciously attacking systems, or disrupting normal network order.
3. You may not reverse engineer, decompile, crack, scrape, or otherwise damage the security or stability of the system.

sections.title: 4. Store and Data Management
paragraphs: 1. Data entered, uploaded, and maintained through the system, including orders, room status, customer information, and channel information, is managed by you and you are responsible for its legality.
2. You must ensure necessary authorization has been obtained for processing relevant information and establish internal permission controls and audit mechanisms.
3. To protect system operations, we will take reasonable technical measures for data isolation, backup, and recovery, but do not promise absolute uninterrupted or error-free service.

sections.title: 5. Fees and Service Changes
paragraphs: 1. For paid services, specific prices, billing rules, and invoice methods are subject to separate agreements or page disclosures.
2. We may optimize or adjust functions, APIs, and billing models based on business development, and will notify you reasonably in advance.

sections.title: 6. Intellectual Property
paragraphs: 1. The intellectual property rights of the system and related software, pages, documents, trademarks, and logos belong to us or relevant rights holders.
2. Without permission, you may not copy, distribute, adapt, sell, license, or otherwise use the above content.

sections.title: 7. Disclaimer and Liability Limitation
paragraphs: 1. For service interruption or data exceptions caused by force majeure, network failures, third-party service exceptions, or regulatory requirements beyond our control, we will assist within a reasonable scope.
2. You are responsible for losses caused by your own reasons, such as account disclosure, mistaken operations, or improper use.
3. To the extent permitted by law, our total liability is capped at the service fees actually paid by you in the twelve months before the relevant dispute occurred.

sections.title: 8. Termination
paragraphs: 1. If you seriously violate these terms or relevant laws, we may suspend or terminate all or part of the services.
2. After termination, unless otherwise required by law, we will process relevant data according to the data retention policy.

sections.title: 9. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
paragraphs: 1. The formation, performance, and interpretation of these terms are governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China.

2. Disputes arising from these terms should first be resolved through friendly negotiation. If negotiation fails, either party may file a lawsuit with a competent court where the platform operator is located.

sections.title: 10. Contact Us
paragraphs: If you have questions about these terms, contact us through in-system customer service or the contact method published by the operator.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how THE HOST HUB collects, uses, stores, shares, and protects your personal information, and how you may exercise related rights.

sections.title: 1. Information We Collect
paragraphs: 1. Account information, such as email, encrypted password, login credentials, and account identifiers.
2. Business information, such as orders, room status, channel configuration, store information, and operation logs.
3. Device and log information, such as IP address, browser type, access time, error logs, and device identifiers.
4. Information you submit voluntarily, such as support inquiries, feedback, and service tickets.

sections.title: 2. How We Use Information
paragraphs: 1. To provide and maintain core system functions, including identity verification, business processing, data synchronization, and notifications.
2. To protect account and system security, including risk identification, troubleshooting, audit tracing, and anomaly alerts.
3. To improve product experience and service quality, including feature optimization, performance analysis, and usage statistics.
4. To fulfill legal obligations and cooperate with lawful requests from competent authorities.

sections.title: 3. Sharing, Transfer, and Disclosure
paragraphs: 1. We do not sell your personal information to unrelated third parties.
2. We may share necessary information in the following situations:
(1) To enable service functions, we may share necessary data with authorized technical service providers.
(2) We may disclose information as required by laws, regulations, judicial, or administrative authorities.
(3) In a merger, division, or asset transfer, we will notify you according to law and require the recipient to continue protection obligations.

sections.title: 4. Cookies and Similar Technologies
paragraphs: To maintain sign-in status and service experience, the system may use cookies or local storage such as localStorage to save necessary session information. You may manage this data through browser settings, but some functions may be affected.

sections.title: 5. Storage and Security
paragraphs: 1. We store and process personal information in China unless otherwise required by laws and regulations.
2. We use access control, permission isolation, transmission encryption, log audits, backup, and recovery measures to protect information security.
3. Although reasonable measures are taken, the Internet is not absolutely secure. Please keep your account and password safe.

sections.title: 6. Your Rights
paragraphs: Where permitted by law, you have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal information, and may request withdrawal of authorization, account cancellation, or copies of relevant information.
After verifying your identity, we will process and respond to your request within a reasonable period.

sections.title: 7. Protection of Minors
paragraphs: The system is mainly intended for business entities with full civil capacity and their authorized personnel. If you are a minor, use the system under guardian guidance and ensure guardian consent has been obtained.

sections.title: 8. Policy Updates
paragraphs: We may update this policy based on business changes or legal requirements. Updates will be announced through system notices or page prompts. Major changes will be presented more prominently.

sections.title: 9. Contact Us
paragraphs: If you have questions, comments, complaints, or suggestions about this Privacy Policy, contact us through in-system customer service or the contact method published by the operator.

support.title: Technical Support
support.subtitle: THE HOST HUB Support
support.intro: If you encounter issues while using THE HOST HUB or need help with account, order, channel, room status, or other functions, contact us through the methods below.

home.subtitle: Review operations and keep check-in review tasks moving from mobile.
home.reviewDescription: Handle registrations, attachment checks, downloads, and review conclusions.
home.reportsDescription: Grouped by daily workflow so managers can open the report they need quickly.
home.focusCards: Mobile statistics are arranged as scannable metric cards and summaries.

home.focusReviews: Review pages keep notes, approvals, rejections, attachments, and history available.

business.label: Business overview
business.note: Core revenue summary for the last 7 days

report.defaultMetricsDescription: Focus on the most important metrics and conclusions for quick scanning.

businessSummary.title: Business summary
businessSummary.subtitle: A compact operating snapshot for owners to scan daily status on mobile.

sections.title: Today
sections.description: Key signals are grouped into a readable operating summary.
bullets: High-rate orders are concentrated in family rooms

sections.title: Follow-up
sections.description: Actionable items stay visible for quick assignment.
bullets: Turn priority items into shift tasks

boundaryNotes: This page focuses on the signals that matter on a phone.
Detailed tables can remain in later drill-down pages.

channelSummary.title: Channel summary
channelSummary.subtitle: Track channel contribution, conversion reminders, and reconciliation priorities.

sections.title: Channel mix
sections.description: Show the channel shares and exceptions owners care about first.
bullets: Direct volume is stable but value trails OTA

sections.title: Mobile display
sections.description: Card summaries keep the conclusion easy to scan.
bullets: Open channel pages for detail when needed
Spacing and hierarchy support one-handed use

boundaryNotes: The page emphasizes business conclusions over dense tables.
More channel metrics can be added later.

notesSummary.title: Transaction note summary
notesSummary.subtitle: Collect manual notes, temporary charges, and follow-up reminders for mobile review.

label: Today records
Missing receipts
Manual income
Exception notes
note: Front desk and duty manager both entered data

sections.title: Fit
sections.description: Desktop fragments are merged into a continuous mobile reading flow.
bullets: Check amount and missing entries first

sections.title: Manager reminders
sections.description: Items needing a second pass are kept in a short list.
bullets: Night cash should include photos before handover
Link exceptions with orders and review actions
Low-frequency history can be enhanced later

boundaryNotes: This page focuses on review and reminders.
The first goal is visibility, discovery, and follow-up.

revenueSummary.title: Revenue summary
revenueSummary.subtitle: Summarize payments, refunds, and AR in a mobile-friendly revenue view.

sections.title: Reconciliation
sections.description: Keep the most-used numbers close to the top.
bullets: Confirm offline payment proof before night audit

sections.title: Display boundary
sections.description: Detailed revenue rows are not expanded on the first mobile screen.
bullets: Leave room for later detail drill-down

boundaryNotes: Core revenue, refund, and AR signals are retained.
Detailed tables can be added later.

operationReport.title: Operations report
operationReport.subtitle: Review occupancy, source mix, and operating alerts for daily manager checks.

sections.title: Reading order
sections.description: Conclusion cards replace long desktop charts.
bullets: Then check channel and member mix

boundaryNotes: Unified report names support daily use.

accommodationReport.title: Accommodation report
accommodationReport.subtitle: Focus on guest mix, room-type distribution, and stay structure.

sections.title: Stay profile
sections.description: Highlight changes in room type and guest mix.
bullets: Twin rooms are close to full

sections.title: Review link
sections.description: Keep semantic links with the review workflow.
bullets: Document gaps are easy to notice here
Pending reviews can be entered from the statistics home

boundaryNotes: Only key accommodation insights are shown first.
The page stays focused for fast mobile review.

financeReport.title: Finance report
financeReport.subtitle: Keep revenue, cost, AR, and alerts visible for mobile decision-making.

sections.title: Finance focus
sections.description: Show decision-oriented items first on mobile.

sections.title: Review focus
sections.description: Control information density for fast scanning.
bullets: Current page solves entry and readability first

boundaryNotes: The finance page focuses on revenue, cost, and AR.
It supports manager mobile inspection first.
